seoTitle: Island Beach vs Island Lagoon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Island Beach and Island Lagoon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Island Beach vs Island Lagoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Island Beach and Island Lagoon.
heading: Island Beach vs Island Lagoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Island Beach, South Australia and Island Lagoon, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Island Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Island Beach (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Island Beach or Island Lagoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Island Beach has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Island Lagoon scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Island Beach scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Island Lagoon scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Island Beach or Island Lagoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Island Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
